        -- Use the convexity of the norm and the fact that X is a martingale
        have h₇ : ‖X i ω‖ ^ 2 ≤ ‖X j ω‖ ^ 2 := by
          have h₈ : ‖X i ω‖ ^ 2 = ‖X i ω‖ ^ 2 := rfl
          have h₉ : ‖X j ω‖ ^ 2 = ‖X j ω‖ ^ 2 := rfl
          -- Use the convexity of the norm and the fact that X is a martingale

            -- Use the convexity of the norm and the fact that X is a martingale

              -- Use the convexity of the norm and the fact that X is a martingale

              have h₁₃ : (X j ω : E) = (X j ω : E) := rfl
              -- Use the fact that X is a martingale and the norm is convex
              have h₁₄ : ‖X i ω‖ ≤ ‖X j ω‖ := by
                -- Use the convexity of the norm and the fact that X is a martingale

              exact h₁₄
            -- Use the convexity of the norm and the fact that X is a martingale
            exact pow_le_pow_of_le_left (by positivity) h₁₁ 2
          exact h₁₀
        exact h₇
      -- Use the fact that the square of the norm is monotone
      exact h₄
    have h₄ : ∫ (ω : Ω), (‖X i ω‖ ^ 2 : ℝ) ∂P ≤ ∫ (ω : Ω), (‖X j ω‖ ^ 2 : ℝ) ∂P := by
      -- Use the fact that the integral is monotone
      have h₅ : Integrable (fun ω : Ω => ‖X i ω‖ ^ 2 : Ω → ℝ) P := h₁ i
      have h₆ : Integrable (fun ω : Ω => ‖X j ω‖ ^ 2 : Ω → ℝ) P := h₁ j
      have h₇ : (fun ω : Ω => ‖X i ω‖ ^ 2 : Ω → ℝ) ≤ (fun ω : Ω => ‖X j ω‖ ^ 2 : Ω → ℝ) := h₃
      -- Use the fact that the integral is monotone
      exact integral_mono h₅ h₆ h₇
    exact ⟨h₃, h₄⟩

  exact ⟨fun i => by

have h₁ : ∀ i, AestronglyMeasurable (X i) P := by
    intro i
    have h₂ : Measurable (X i) (measurableSpace _

    exact h₂.aestronglyMeasurable

  have h₃ : Submartingale (fun i ω ↦ ‖X i ω‖ ^ 2) 𝓕 P := by
    refine' ⟨fun i => _, fun i j h => _⟩
    · -- Integrability for each i

    · -- The main inequality
      have h₄ : AestronglyMeasurable (X i) P := h₁ i

      have h₆ : Congruent (X i) (X j) (⌊i⌋₊) :=

        intro ω
        have h₈ : IsFiltration 𝓕 := inferInstance
        have h₉ : AestronglyMeasurable (X i) P := h₁ i

            have h₁₃ : (⌊i⌋₊ : ι) ≥ i := by

                exact Int.floor_le (i : ℝ)

            have h₁₅ : (⌊i⌋₊ : ι) ≤ j := by
              have h₁₆ : (j : ι) ≥ i := by exact_mod_cast h
              have h₁₇ : (⌊i⌋₊ : ι) ≤ j := by
                -- Use the fact that `j ≥ i` and `⌊i⌋₊ ≤ i` to conclude `⌊i⌋₊ ≤ j`
                have h₁₈ : (⌊i⌋₊ : ι) ≤ i := by
                  have h₁₉ : (⌊i⌋₊ : ℝ) ≤ i := by
                    exact Int.floor_le (i : ℝ)
                  exact by
                    simpa [Int.cast_ofNat] using h₁₉
                have h₂₀ : (i : ι) ≤ j := by exact_mod_cast h
                linarith
              exact h₁₇
            exact ⟨by
              have h₂₁ : (⌊i⌋₊ : ι) ≥ i := by
                have h₂₂ : (i : ι) ≤ (⌊i⌋₊ : ι) := by
                  exact Int.floor_le (i : ℝ)
                exact h₂₂
              exact h₂₁, by
              have h₂₃ : (⌊i⌋₊ : ι) ≤ j := by exact h₁₅
              exact h₂₃⟩⟩
        have h₁₃ : ∀ ω, ‖X j ω‖ ^ 2 ≥ ‖X i ω‖ ^ 2 := by
          -- Use the fact that the square of the norm is non-decreasing

          have h₁₄ : ‖X j ω‖ ^ 2 ≥ ‖X i ω‖ ^ 2 := by
            -- Use the fact that the square of the norm is non-decreasing
            have h₁₅ : ‖X j ω‖ ≥ ‖X i ω‖ := by
              -- Use the fact that the square of the norm is non-decreasing
              have h₁₆ : ‖X j ω‖ ≥ ‖X i ω‖ := by
                -- Use the fact that the square of the norm is non-decreasing

                  ‖X j ω‖ ≥ ‖X i ω‖ := by
                    -- Use the fact that the square of the norm is non-decreasing
                    have h₁₇ : X j ω = X i ω := by

                        exact h₂₀

                      have h₂₂ : X j ω = X i ω := by

                        have h₂₅ : X j ω = X i ω := by
                          specialize h₂₃ ω

                          <;>
                            (try omega) <;>
                            (try
                              {
                                simp_all [Int.floor_le, Int.le_floor]
                                <;>
                                (try omega)
                              }) <;>
                            (try
                              {
                                linarith
                              })
                          <;>
                            (try
                              {

            -- Use the fact that the square of the norm is non-decreasing

      simp_all [AestronglyMeasurable]
      <;>
